candidates.notes: DC archetype-5 MIXED-RECORD MAYOR — THREE significant rightward deviations on policing (TRUE q0/q1/q2/q4), economic stewardship (TRUE q2 — FY26 Grow-DC repeals Initiative 82 tipped-wage + no tax hikes + business-friendly), and border (TRUE q2 — May 2025 FY26 budget proposed REPEAL of Sanctuary Values Act MPD-ICE-cooperation bar, Council removed provision) layered over conventional progressive base on life/marriage/family/election/2A/christian-liberty. candidates.name: Eleanor Holmes Norton
candidates.office: Delegate to U.S. House (non-voting) · (retiring · seat open 2026)
candidates.notes: Serving since 1991, age 88. CURRENCY FIX 2026-06-09: after publicly insisting through mid-Jan 2026 she would run, her campaign filed an FEC termination report on 2026-01-25 ending the 2026 reelection bid; she is retiring at the end of this term and is NOT on the 2026 ballot (WaPo/NPR/NOTUS). Still holds the seat through the 119th Congress. Signature issue is DC statehood (H.R. 51, passed House 2020 and 2021). Uniformly progressive record: SBA Pro-Life notes she consistently voted against protections for the unborn; original Equality Act cosponsor and founding member of the Transgender Equality Task Force; decades-long opponent of the DC Opportunity Scholarship voucher program (supports charters as the home-rule alternative); introduced a bill to END RFRA's applicability to DC; career defender of DC's assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, and may-issue carry regime; defended DC's sanctuary law and student COVID-vaccine mandate against congressional disapproval. No conservative cross-party deviations of substance found.
